reasons: Integration diagnostics flag 100% malformed list encodings in fused columns _fusion_sources and _fusion_source_datasets (2000/2000 sampled each), indicating broken list canonicalization and hurting lineage/auditing.

Evaluation reasoning notes secondary but real canonicalization/alias issues in categorical text fields (publisher/developer abbreviations like SCEA vs Sony Computer Entertainment; TT Fusion vs Traveller’s Tales) that can cause exact-match evaluation misses when clusters are otherwise correct.
ESRB representation may be inconsistent with gold expectations (e.g., M17+ vs M), suggesting value-level normalization should be aligned to validation style rather than collapsing categories.
normalization_directives.recommendation_summary: Rerun normalization primarily to fix malformed list-like lineage fields (_fusion_sources, _fusion_source_datasets) into a consistent JSON-array encoding; additionally apply light, gold-aligned canonicalization for publisher/developer aliases and ensure ESRB values match validation-set conventions (avoid case changes beyond lowercasing id).
